title: Panel heartbeat status.json
description: The shared contract for the collected multi-agent TUI heartbeat file. Both producers conform to it: the Skill Audit Loop panel-enrich runner (lib/audit/panel-progress.js, via createProgressReporter().heartbeat()) and the workspace /boardmeeting Phase 2 writer (scripts/agent/boardmeeting-heartbeat.js). The viewer scripts/watch-panel.js reads this file and paints renderCollected(); keeping both producers on one schema means /boardmeeting and /skill-audit-loop render the SAME collected view. A producer MAY emit additional fields the viewer ignores; this schema names the required core plus the optional fields the renderer reads.

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of this heartbeat write (e.g. new Date().toISOString()).

pid.description: Process id of the producer, for liveness checks by the watch wrappers.

skill.description: The label rendered in the collected-view header. For the panel-enrich loop this is the skill name; for /boardmeeting it is the meeting label (e.g. 'Sales Hub board (dev)'). The renderer reads `skill` for both producers, so the field name is shared even though the meaning is the producer's subject.

phase.description: Current phase label of the whole run (e.g. 'dispatch', 'propose (advisory)', 'review', 'complete').

elapsed_s.description: Whole-run elapsed seconds since the producer started. Optional; the renderer shows it in the header when present.

total.description: Total number of agents in the panel.

done.description: Count of agents in a terminal-success state.

failed.description: Count of agents in a terminal-failure state (failed or timeout).

running.description: In-flight cells (one per actively-running agent unit). Optional; the watch wrappers use it for stall detection.

cell.description: Identifier for the running unit (e.g. 'minimax:propose' or a model alias).

elapsed_s.description: Seconds this cell has been running.

complete.description: True once every agent has reached a terminal state. The viewer treats this as the COMPLETE terminal signal.

agents.description: One entry per panel agent (mandatory + advisory), in display order. This is what renderCollected() paints as the agent tree.

model.description: Model alias/role (e.g. 'opus', 'gpt-5.5', 'minimax'). The renderer resolves it to a full display name.

tier.description: Panel tier. 'mandatory' and 'quality' are the certifying/quality tiers; 'advisory' is breadth-only. The renderer groups 'mandatory'/'quality' against 'advisory' in the summary line.

phase.description: The agent's current phase (e.g. 'propose', 'review', 'revise'), or null before it starts.

state.description: The agent's lifecycle state. Drives the row glyph in the collected view.

elapsed_s.description: Seconds this agent has been in its current active state (0 when not actively running).

failure_reason.description: On a failed/skipped agent, the distinct reason: 'timeout' (ran past the dispatch budget — often a free model web-search-looping), 'error' (auth/launch/crash), or 'no-document' (dispatch ok but no usable output). Null otherwise. Lets a buried advisory failure stay visible instead of collapsing to the ✗/– glyph (F4).
